column2: National Certificate in IT: Systems Development
column16: SAQA ID: 48872. 131 credits. 1-year programme. No Maths % requirement beyond NSC pass. Accredited by MICT SETA. Purely vocational — focuses on systems development
column17: software design and testing. Gateway into Diploma in IT (serves as stepping stone for students who don't meet Diploma entry). No Maths Literacy or Technical Maths distinction made — requirement is passing grade only.

column5: Higher Certificate pass + NQF Level 5 qualification
column15: SAQA ID: 71850. 120 credits. 1-year programme. Requires prior NQF Level 5 qualification (e.g. National Certificate in IT). Not directly accessible from matric — students must first complete National Certificate in IT. Focuses on database development
column18: business data solutions. Accredited by MICT SETA.

column14: SAQA ID: 120097. 360 credits. 2.5-year academic + 6 months workplace training = 3 years total. Pure Mathematics 50% minimum. Maths Literacy and Technical Maths NOT accepted — only Pure Mathematics. English not specified as minimum % for Diploma (only for degrees). Two streams: Software Development (sub-specialisations: Programming
column19: Network). Mathematics Bridging Course not available for Diploma — only for degrees.

column2: Diploma in Information Technology for Deaf Students
column14: Same qualification as Diploma in IT (SAQA ID: 120097
column16: 360 credits) — specialised delivery model for Deaf and hard-of-hearing students. 4-year programme (extended from standard 3 years). South African Sign Language (SASL) interpreters provided throughout including workplace training. Software Development specialisation. Half-moon seating arrangement. Deaf students also now accepted into Bachelor of IT. Pure Mathematics 50% required.

column14: SAQA ID: 94121. 360 credits. 3-year full-time programme. Pure Mathematics 50% minimum — Maths Literacy and Technical Maths NOT accepted. English 50% minimum. Mathematics Bridging Course available: students who didn't take Pure Maths or scored below 50% can attend Bridging Course
column15: must achieve 50% to gain entry. Specialises in Software Development. Covers software engineering
column19: game development. Deaf students now also accepted.

column14: Same qualification as Bachelor of IT (SAQA ID: 94121
column16: 360 credits). Same admission requirements: Bachelor's pass
column18: Pure Maths 50%. Classes on Saturdays only (8am–5pm). 5-year programme (same content as full-time BIT stretched over 5 years). For working professionals. RPL accepted — prior IT qualifications/experience may shorten the programme.

column14: SAQA ID: 62689. 506 credits. NQF Level 8 — professional degree (4 years). Pure Mathematics 50% required at NSC level. Maths Literacy and Technical Maths NOT accepted. Mathematics Bridging Course available for students who did not take Pure Maths or scored below 50%
column15: must achieve 70% in Bridging Course to gain entry into BComp (vs 50% for BIT). English 50% minimum. 3 years academic + 1 year workplace training (internship at national or international company) + dissertation. Specialisations: Data Science OR Software Engineering. Internationally recognised — benchmarked against KU Leuven and UAntwerp (Belgium). Over 8% of SA ICT graduates come from Belgium Campus.
